hard.next_step: Feb 20, 2026: POC in progress Jan 22, 2026: POC is being planned, Caroline proposed an onsite meeting while in SA On Nov 25, 2025 Santam shared their NDA and Menachem has forwarded it to our legal team for their review Today Nov 27, Menachem sent a follow up email to Legal requesting feedback Next steps: Following the NDA review, we'll proceed to book a follow up discussion with the prospect.
hard.ais_why:
• Mar 2026 Avoma notes focus on a custom claims-excess processing application built on the Zycus platform; AI is not a topic in any captured conversation. Deal scope includes Merlin alongside eProcurement, iContract, and iSupplier ‚Äî AI products are present (Merlin) but not driving conversation. The functional discussion is anchored in insurance-claims-specific workflow configuration.
• Formal Evaluation with BD Engaged and very high meeting cadence (14 meetings Oct 2025 - Mar 2026) with stable Santam stakeholders (Daphney Mvelase, Mpfuxi Makondo, Ashlee Naidoo, Hendrico Kokum, Zodwa Moshanyana) and active Zycus team. The meeting topics centre on functional gaps (claims excess processing) rather than AI use cases. Last activity Mar 31, 2026.
• Medium confidence: clear meeting evidence (14 calls) but topic centers on functional/workflow configuration rather than AI. Merlin in scope is not enough to push above 5/6 boundary without buyer-spoken AI language. Default DOWN at 5/6 held; would move UP if Merlin/POC discussion surfaces in later meetings or if a Phase 2 emerges with AI-specific scope.

north_star_verdict.headline: Santam has gone dark: the last real buyer touch was the 31 Mar 2026 POC reschedule, the stage was pushed back to Initial Interest on 26 May before a 1 Jul requalification, and the 30 Sep close is 86 days out with no engaged economic buyer.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-10-09, 270 days old. Stage Qualified (early tier), re-set on 2026-07-01. Last Salesforce activity 2026-03-31, ~97 days ago. The 10/17/24 Mar POC checkpoints were the last genuine buyer touches; the 31 Mar note only rescheduled and no meeting followed. Stage regressed Formal Evaluation to Initial Interest on 26 May, then Initial Interest to Qualified on 1 Jul, so the evaluation was effectively restarted, not advanced. With 86 days to a $200K close and roughly three months of buyer silence, the deal needs re-qualification and a compressed eval before the close date is credible.
evidence: Last Salesforce activity 2026-03-31 (~97 days ago); no buyer-facing deliverable executed since
Stage regressed Formal Evaluation -> Initial Interest on 2026-05-26 (Caroline Lacocque), then -> Qualified on 2026-07-01
POC was 'in progress' per the 20 Feb 2026 next step but has no recorded completion or outcome
1 Jul 2026 Opportunity Health tasks flag 'Decision Maker is not identified' and 'Low Prospect Inbound Communication'
No Avoma transcripts retrieved this sweep and no competitor named in any source

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case is on record. The functional pain is real and specific: the Mar 2026 Avoma notes (per AIS_Why__c) centre on a custom claims-excess processing application built on the Zycus platform, which points to insurance-claims workflow automation as the driver. But no cost, cycle-time, headcount or savings figure has been captured in any field or activity, and the Description is empty. For a $200K S2C deal (iContract, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin) the buyer would normally state contract-leakage, supplier-onboarding-time or maverick-spend targets; none exist. The move is to reconvene the procurement team and quantify the claims-excess and S2C pain in writing.
sources: SF AIS_Why__c (Mar 2026 Avoma notes reference)
SF Description: empty
SF Products__c: iContract;iSupplier;eProcurement;Merlin
No Avoma transcript read this sweep

economic_buyer.narrative: Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation) is on the opportunity contact roles and his title makes him the natural budget owner for a group-sourcing transformation, so the economic buyer is mapped by name and seniority. What is missing is engagement: there is no recorded call, email or next step showing Johan Human personally in the commercial conversation, and the 1 Jul 2026 health task explicitly flags 'Decision Maker is not identified.' Aslam Higgins (Head of Procurement) is the likely decision maker alongside him. The move is to earn a direct working session with Johan Human and Aslam Higgins to reconfirm sponsorship and budget.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Johan Human, Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation
SF OpportunityContactRole: Aslam Higgins, Head of Procurement
SF Task 2026-07-01 'Decision Maker is not identified — Opportunity Health'

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are inferred from the POC scope, not confirmed by a quoted buyer statement this sweep. The Mar 2026 checkpoints were POC checkpoints on a custom claims-excess processing application, so the buyer is clearly validating fit for insurance-specific claims workflow on the Zycus platform, plus the standard S2C footprint (contract, supplier, procurement). No formal BRD, use-case scorecard or weighting has been captured, and no Avoma transcript was available to quote the buyer's stated must-haves. The move is to retrieve the POC success criteria and confirm them in writing with the procurement team.
sources: SF Activities: 'SANTAM POC Checkpoint' meetings 2026-03-10, 2026-03-17, 2026-03-24
SF AIS_Why__c (claims-excess processing focus)
No Avoma transcript read this sweep

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is only partly visible and has recently gone backwards. The call sequence shows a series of POC checkpoints through March 2026, then the stage was pushed from the relevant stakeholder back to the relevant stakeholder on 26 May and re-qualified on 1 Jul, which signals the evaluation stalled and was reset rather than progressing toward a decision. No approval chain, evaluation timeline or committee structure is documented, and the 1 Jul health tasks include 'Get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process.' The move is to map the evaluation stages and the approval path with Marcel Rose and Aslam Higgins.
sources: SF Field History: StageName Formal Evaluation -> Initial Interest 2026-05-26; Initial Interest -> Qualified 2026-07-01
SF Task 2026-07-01 'Get clarity on the evaluation stages and influence the process'

paper_process.narrative: There is early paper-process signal but no forward motion. Santam shared their NDA on 25 Nov 2025 and Zycus legal was engaged (follow-up to Legal 27 Nov 2025), which shows Santam runs a formal legal gate before contracting. There is no record of the NDA outcome, no MSA/SOW scoping, and no InfoSec or data-privacy review, all of which a BFSI SaaS procurement deal will require. As a new-business full-suite S2C deal this will need an MSA plus Order Form and a separately-signed SOW. The move is to confirm the NDA status and outline the contracting path once the eval restarts.
sources: SF Next_Step__c: NDA shared 2025-11-25; legal follow-up 2025-11-27
No Avoma transcript read this sweep

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is identified qualitatively but not owned or quantified. The March POC targeted a custom claims-excess processing application on the Zycus platform (per AIS_Why__c), so the operational pain is insurance-claims processing and broader source-to-contract inefficiency across contract, supplier and procurement management. What is missing is a named pain owner tying the pain to a KPI (for example Aslam Higgins owning maverick-spend or Johan Human owning transformation targets) and any cost-of-inaction figure. The move is to confirm the pain owner and the business consequence of not solving it.
sources: SF AIS_Why__c (claims-excess processing application, Mar 2026)
SF Products__c: iContract;iSupplier;eProcurement;Merlin

champion.narrative: No champion has been secured. Marcel Rose (Procurement Manager) was the working contact through NDA and POC planning (Nov 2025 to Feb 2026) and is the most frequently referenced Santam name, but there is no evidence of champion behaviour: no advocacy, no facilitation of access to Johan Human or Aslam Higgins, no competitive push-back, and no engagement since 31 Mar 2026. Daphney Mvelase (Non-Motor Desktop Assessing Manager) appears as an operational/end-user stakeholder, not a sponsor. The ~97-day silence means any early relationship has cooled. The move is to re-engage Marcel Rose to test his disposition and, through him, build a sponsor with access to the economic buyer.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Marcel Rose, Procurement Manager
SF Next_Step__c (Marcel Rose during NDA/POC phase)
SF LastActivityDate 2026-03-31
No Champion role flagged on any contact

competition.narrative: No competitor is named in any source: Competitors__c is empty, the free-text competition fields carry nothing, no Avoma transcript was read this sweep, and no next step or task references a rival, RFP round or shortlist. On a 270-day BFSI deal that has already run a POC this is a genuine knowledge gap, not evidence of sole-sourcing; a South African group insurer of Santam's size would typically benchmark alternatives before contracting. The move is to ask Marcel Rose or Aslam Higgins directly whether other vendors are in the evaluation and what the current stack is, so the field stops being a blind spot.
sources: SF Competitors__c: empty
SF free-text competition fields: no value
No Avoma transcript read this sweep
SF Next_Step__c / Tasks: no competitor reference

deal_movement.summary: Momentum peaked with the March POC checkpoints, then reversed: the stage was regressed to Initial Interest on 26 May and re-qualified on 1 Jul with no fresh buyer engagement, so the deal is effectively restarting from cold rather than advancing.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source this sweep or on the prior record, so the competitive field is genuinely unknown rather than sole-sourced. On a 270-day BFSI S2C deal that has already run a POC, that absence is a critical blind spot: a group insurer of Santam's scale usually benchmarks alternatives (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP and Jaggaer are the common South African enterprise field) before contracting. The dominant current threat is not a named rival but no-decision inertia, given ~97 days of silence and a reset evaluation. Discovering the true competitive context is a top requalification move; no rival is asserted without evidence.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit is centred on insurance-specific claims workflow: Santam is validating a custom claims-excess processing application on the Zycus platform alongside the standard source-to-contract suite. Alignment is unconfirmed this sweep because no POC outcome or buyer quote was captured.

best_practice_check.summary: This is an early-stage BFSI deal that has cooled and reset; the win levers are re-engagement, power access and discovering the unknown competitive field before the 30 Sep close loses credibility.
flags: Rebuild the thread beyond Marcel Rose: pull Johan Human and Aslam Higgins into a reset session, since the eval cannot advance without engaged power.
Discover the competitive field you have never captured; on a 270-day BFSI S2C deal, unknown alternatives are the single biggest blind spot.
Reconfirm the POC outcome and secure a buyer-owned date; without a compelling event the 30 Sep close is a placeholder, not a plan.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Santam went dark after the March POC checkpoints: the stage was pushed back to Initial Interest on 26 May then re-qualified on 1 Jul with no buyer contact since 31 Mar, and neither the economic buyer Johan Human nor any champion is engaged on a $200K S2C deal 86 days from its 30 Sep close.

win_position.text: Weak position: no buyer contact since 31 Mar 2026, and the stage was reset from Formal Evaluation back to Initial Interest on 26 May before a 1 Jul requalification, so momentum has to be rebuilt from scratch.

No power is engaged: Johan Human (Executive Head: Group Sourcing & Transformation) is on the contact roles but has no recorded personal touch, and the 1 Jul health flag itself reads 'Decision Maker is not identified'.
No competitor is named anywhere, which on a 270-day BFSI deal is an unmeasured blind spot rather than a sole-source win.

commentary.win_position: Win 11 is carried by momentum 55 vs stage-expected 50; exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 55 (moving forward): Next Step carries 4 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (11), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 55, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
